questions.text: What does “to read the room” mean? [AUDIO: https://s3.academy-ai.kz/magistratura-media/uploads/10dc0a45-95d1-41e2-bc6e-0a7e3c0be1d0.mp3]

payload.text: What does “to read the room” mean?

options.text: To read a book while sitting in a room.
To ask everyone in a group what they are feeling.
To observe a group and understand its general mood.
To change the furniture in a room.

payload.text: What are moods?

options.text: Emotional states such as happy, sad, angry, or anxious.

payload.text: According to the audio, who can have a mood?

options.text: Both individuals and groups of people.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: What should you do if you enter a serious work meeting while feeling silly? [AUDIO: https://s3.academy-ai.kz/magistratura-media/uploads/10dc0a45-95d1-41e2-bc6e-0a7e3c0be1d0.mp3]

payload.text: What should you do if you enter a serious work meeting while feeling silly?

options.text: Read the room and change your behavior.
Ask everyone to become less serious.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: When might the speaker wait before bringing up a serious issue with friends? [AUDIO: https://s3.academy-ai.kz/magistratura-media/uploads/10dc0a45-95d1-41e2-bc6e-0a7e3c0be1d0.mp3]

payload.text: When might the speaker wait before bringing up a serious issue with friends?

options.text: When everyone is having a good time and the issue is not urgent.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: Why might someone tell another person to “read the room”? [AUDIO: https://s3.academy-ai.kz/magistratura-media/uploads/10dc0a45-95d1-41e2-bc6e-0a7e3c0be1d0.mp3]

payload.text: Why might someone tell another person to “read the room”?

options.text: Because they are reading too slowly.
Because they are behaving in a way that does not match the group’s mood.
Because they forgot to attend a meeting.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: How can the phrase “read the room” sound? [AUDIO: https://s3.academy-ai.kz/magistratura-media/uploads/10dc0a45-95d1-41e2-bc6e-0a7e3c0be1d0.mp3]

payload.text: How can the phrase “read the room” sound?

options.text: Funny or insulting, depending on the situation and tone.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: What happens when Betsy brings up a serious topic at the dinner party? [AUDIO: https://s3.academy-ai.kz/magistratura-media/uploads/10dc0a45-95d1-41e2-bc6e-0a7e3c0be1d0.mp3]

payload.text: What happens when Betsy brings up a serious topic at the dinner party?

options.text: The tone changes from happy to serious.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: What kind of people are good at reading the room? [AUDIO: https://s3.academy-ai.kz/magistratura-media/uploads/10dc0a45-95d1-41e2-bc6e-0a7e3c0be1d0.mp3]

payload.text: What kind of people are good at reading the room?

options.text: People who avoid all serious conversations.
People who are observant and can fit in with the atmosphere, tone, and mood.
People who speak the loudest in a group.

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: Read the text and choose the correct answer. Earth Hour. Earth Hour is a worldwide campaign held every year. It asks people, companies, and organizations in different countries to turn off unnecessary electric lights at the same time for one hour. The first Earth Hour took place in Australia in 2007, when 2.2 million people in Sydney switched off non-essential lights for sixty minutes. Since that time, the campaign has become a very large international event. In 2013, millions of people in 7,000 cities and towns around the world turned off their lights for one hour near the end of March. Famous landmarks and buildings also join the event every year. Places such as the Sydney Opera House, the Harbour Bridge, the Petronas Towers in Malaysia, the UK Parliament, Buckingham Palace, and the Empire State Building all go dark for Earth Hour. The campaign even reached space in 2011, when astronauts reduced power on the International Space Station. Earth Hour is organized by the World Wide Fund for Nature, or WWF. The idea was created by Andy Ridley, who was originally from Britain and became the executive director of Earth Hour Global. He wanted people to become more aware of environmental problems by doing something positive for the planet. Turning off lights for one hour can reduce energy use a little, but the event is also symbolic. It encourages people to think about climate change. The end of March is close to the spring equinox in the northern hemisphere and the autumn equinox in the southern hemisphere. At this time, sunset happens at similar times in both parts of the world. Because of this, a global “lights out” event is most visually powerful at this period of the year. In 2013, in the UK, the pop group McFly performed live acoustic music without electricity while wearing panda costumes, because the WWF logo is a panda. Famous chefs Gordon Ramsay and Raymond Blanc created special recipes for families to cook and eat by candlelight. Actors and TV personalities, including Kevin McCloud and Miranda Richardson, recorded Rudyard Kipling’s Just So Stories so that families could listen to them by candlelight. Who created the idea of Earth Hour?

payload.text: Read the text and choose the correct answer. Earth Hour. Earth Hour is a worldwide campaign held every year. It asks people, companies, and organizations in different countries to turn off unnecessary electric lights at the same time for one hour. The first Earth Hour took place in Australia in 2007, when 2.2 million people in Sydney switched off non-essential lights for sixty minutes. Since that time, the campaign has become a very large international event. In 2013, millions of people in 7,000 cities and towns around the world turned off their lights for one hour near the end of March. Famous landmarks and buildings also join the event every year. Places such as the Sydney Opera House, the Harbour Bridge, the Petronas Towers in Malaysia, the UK Parliament, Buckingham Palace, and the Empire State Building all go dark for Earth Hour. The campaign even reached space in 2011, when astronauts reduced power on the International Space Station. Earth Hour is organized by the World Wide Fund for Nature, or WWF. The idea was created by Andy Ridley, who was originally from Britain and became the executive director of Earth Hour Global. He wanted people to become more aware of environmental problems by doing something positive for the planet. Turning off lights for one hour can reduce energy use a little, but the event is also symbolic. It encourages people to think about climate change. The end of March is close to the spring equinox in the northern hemisphere and the autumn equinox in the southern hemisphere. At this time, sunset happens at similar times in both parts of the world. Because of this, a global “lights out” event is most visually powerful at this period of the year. In 2013, in the UK, the pop group McFly performed live acoustic music without electricity while wearing panda costumes, because the WWF logo is a panda. Famous chefs Gordon Ramsay and Raymond Blanc created special recipes for families to cook and eat by candlelight. Actors and TV personalities, including Kevin McCloud and Miranda Richardson, recorded Rudyard Kipling’s Just So Stories so that families could listen to them by candlelight. Who created the idea of Earth Hour?

questions.subject: Иностранный язык(Английский язык)
questions.text: Read the text and choose the correct answer. Earth Hour. Earth Hour is a worldwide campaign held every year. It asks people, companies, and organizations in different countries to turn off unnecessary electric lights at the same time for one hour. The first Earth Hour took place in Australia in 2007, when 2.2 million people in Sydney switched off non-essential lights for sixty minutes. Since that time, the campaign has become a very large international event. In 2013, millions of people in 7,000 cities and towns around the world turned off their lights for one hour near the end of March. Famous landmarks and buildings also join the event every year. Places such as the Sydney Opera House, the Harbour Bridge, the Petronas Towers in Malaysia, the UK Parliament, Buckingham Palace, and the Empire State Building all go dark for Earth Hour. The campaign even reached space in 2011, when astronauts reduced power on the International Space Station. Earth Hour is organized by the World Wide Fund for Nature, or WWF. The idea was created by Andy Ridley, who was originally from Britain and became the executive director of Earth Hour Global. He wanted people to become more aware of environmental problems by doing something positive for the planet. Turning off lights for one hour can reduce energy use a little, but the event is also symbolic. It encourages people to think about climate change. The end of March is close to the spring equinox in the northern hemisphere and the autumn equinox in the southern hemisphere. At this time, sunset happens at similar times in both parts of the world. Because of this, a global “lights out” event is most visually powerful at this period of the year. In 2013, in the UK, the pop group McFly performed live acoustic music without electricity while wearing panda costumes, because the WWF logo is a panda. Famous chefs Gordon Ramsay and Raymond Blanc created special recipes for families to cook and eat by candlelight. Actors and TV personalities, including Kevin McCloud and Miranda Richardson, recorded Rudyard Kipling’s Just So Stories so that families could listen to them by candlelight. Who created the idea of Earth Hour?
